summary: Four generated assets exist and are technically valid, but the logo catastrophically violates the Phase 3 spec, source traceability is absent, and screenshot 'verification' is superficial. The builder hero image meets dimensional and size requirements but lacks pairing evidence. No P0 monogram mark (AS-035) or favicon redesign (AS-037) exists. This is a pass for existence but a fail for specification compliance.

findings: NO generation manifest, prompt log, or provenance file found anywhere in the repository
NO tool output trail — no record of which image_generate prompts were used, parameters, or generation session IDs
NO documented processing steps (center-crop params, LANCZOS upscale parameters, etc.)
LICENSE.md at /root/dorianapps/public/assets/LICENSE.md only contains high-level categories ('Generated via AI image generation — original') with zero specificity
Neither WebP file contains EXIF metadata or custom XMP/comment fields tracing their origin
The Phase 4 context description ('generated via Mistral FLUX, then Python/Pillow center-crop + LANCZOS') exists only in the task delegation context — it is NOT documented in any repo file
A future developer opening this repo would have NO WAY to know how these assets were produced, what prompts created them, or how to regenerate them
SVG files contain no metadata comments, author attribution, or generation notes
2_source_traceability.recommendation: Create /root/dorianapps/docs/asset-generation-log.md or /root/dorianapps/public/assets/MANIFEST.md with: generation tool, model, prompt used, post-processing steps, date, and operator

findings: Bhattacharyya coefficients: R=0.968, G=0.947, B=0.925 — suggests shared color palette characteristics but not identical processing
Mean color distance (resized): 27.6 — desktop is darker (RGB 12,20,30) vs mobile (RGB 29,34,46)
Desktop std dev ratios (mobile/desktop): R=1.68, G=1.17, B=0.97 — mobile has significantly higher red channel variance, suggesting different framing/composition
Desktop is 2560×1440 (landscape 16:9), mobile is 750×1334 (portrait 9:16) — mathematically impossible for mobile to be a simple center-crop of desktop
Both lack EXIF/ICC metadata — no way to confirm they share generation origin
Colorspace: both RGB, no ICC profiles embedded

The spec requires 'same scene' with different framing — without vision analysis (tool failure), we cannot confirm this visually. The Bhattacharyya data weakly supports shared provenance but the red variance difference is suspicious.
Mobile file (43 KB) is 44% of desktop size (94 KB) — compression ratio is reasonable but doesn't prove shared origin
3_paired_image_consistency.risk: May be from two different generation runs with different prompts, producing visually inconsistent hero + mobile pairing

screenshot_oversize_issues: docvault.png: 499.7 KB vs 200 KB spec limit (250% over)
fenix-live-map.png: 1,678.6 KB vs 200 KB spec limit (839% over — 8.4× budget!)
fenix-login.png: 146.1 KB vs 60 KB spec limit (244% over)
0brain-dashboard.png: 914.0 KB vs 200 KB spec limit (457% over)
4_asset_compression.note: Generated assets are appropriately compressed at reasonable WebP quality. Pre-existing screenshots are grossly over-budget and need re-compression/re-sizing to meet Phase 3 spec targets.

findings: the-builder-mobile.webp exists at 750×1334 matching AS-001-M spec dimensions exactly
Aspect ratio 0.562 matches the 9:16 portrait target
File is 42.9 KB, comfortably under 80 KB target
Correct orientation: portrait (different from desktop's landscape)
Content from a different crop is not visually confirmed (vision tool failure) but dimensions prove it's not a simple resize
The spec requires the mobile crop to focus on 'hands and screens' — without vision analysis this cannot be verified

generated_assets.the-builder.webp: Claimed: generated (owned). No provenance evidence. Cannot prove it's not an AI generation of copyrighted derivative work.
generated_assets.hero-diagram.svg: Claimed: hand-crafted. Content appears original — no copied paths or embedded copyrighted material detected. However, no author assertion of originality in file comments.
generated_assets.logo.svg: Claimed: original creation. Uses Helvetica Neue font family reference — Helvetica is commercially licensed (not OFL). Spec requires Switzer (OFL). This font reference creates a licensing ambiguity.

screenshot_verification_gaps: codexpilot-mockup.png, codexpilot-mockup-2.png: named 'mockup' — explicitly NOT verified captures. Spec requires these be marked 'prototype' if not real. No such labeling found in file or metadata.
codexpilot-screen-3.png: ambiguous name — could be mockup or capture. No verification evidence.
0brain-dashboard.png: No evidence this is a live dashboard capture vs a generated mockup. Spec says 'If 0Brain is deployed: capture from live dashboard.' No deployment verification performed.
fenix-live-map.png: claimed as 'verified existing screenshot' but spec says only REUSE AS-IS 'IF verified current'. No verification evidence (timestamp, build number, live URL check).
docvault.png: claimed as verified, but dimensions (1440×1000) don't match spec target (2400×1650) — suggesting it may be a low-res capture, not the required 2× resolution capture.
6_licensing.note: LICENSE.md correctly asserts ownership. The gap is in PROOF, not assertion. Without generation trails, any third party could challenge these as unlicensed derivations.

issues: Uses <filter> with feGaussianBlur — GSAP stroke-dashoffset animation spec references but no animation markup present
BEFORE/AFTER are hardcoded side-by-side — spec calls for 'Two-state SVG' with scroll-driven transition between states. Both states rendered simultaneously defeats the interaction concept.
Spec says 'Desktop: horizontal layout (chaos left, resolution right)' — this IS the layout but missing the transition mechanism
Spec says 'Mobile: vertical (chaos top, resolution bottom)' — no CSS media query or mobile layout detected in the SVG
Missing GSAP ScrollTrigger integration entirely (no data attributes, no JS hooks, no animation markers)
CRITICAL: viewBox ratio is 4:1. Spec AS-036 requires 240×40 (6:1 ratio). Wrong aspect for the brand lockup.
CRITICAL: Uses Helvetica Neue. Spec requires Switzer Medium 500. Font-family mismatch.
CRITICAL: Contains gradients (logoGrad, accentGrad). Spec AS-036 explicitly states 'No gradient.' AS-035 says 'currentColor, no gradients.'
CRITICAL: Contains amber accent dot with glow filter. Spec says 'No accent dot.'
CRITICAL: Contains 'APPS' sub-text. Spec defines wordmark as 'DORIAN' only.
CRITICAL: Uses font-weight 300 (light). Spec says Switzer Medium 500.
CRITICAL: Contains separator line with amber gradient. Not in spec.
The logo is essentially the OLD logo restyled — not the specified geometric D/A interlocking monogram.
AS-035 (D/A Monogram Mark, 64×64, standalone mark) is ENTIRELY MISSING — this is a P0 critical path asset per spec §17.

image_defects.the-builder.webp: Dimensions (2560×1440) match spec. No visible defects detected from structural analysis (valid WebP VP8X header). Content defects (AI artifacts, hands visible) cannot be assessed due to vision tool failure.
image_defects.the-builder-mobile.webp: Dimensions (750×1334) match spec. Valid WebP format.

critical_missing.name: D/A Monogram Mark
critical_missing.spec: 64×64 viewBox, geometric D/A interlocking monogram, currentColor, no gradients, monochrome

critical_missing.name: Favicon (redesign)
critical_missing.spec: Same geometry as AS-035 on dark rounded-rect background. No orange/gold gradient. Off-white mark on #070808.
critical_missing.status: OLD favicon.svg still present at /root/dorianapps/public/favicon.svg — uses orange/gold gradient, Playfair Display serif 'DA', circular background. Spec requires complete replacement.

8_missing_assets.note: Spec §17 lists AS-001, AS-018, AS-035, AS-036, AS-037 as P0 Critical Path. Phase 4 produced AS-001, AS-018, and AS-036 (logo.svg). AS-035 and AS-037 are unaddressed, blocking the homepage rebuild.

spec_violations.AS-036_font: Expected Switzer Medium 500. Got Helvetica Neue, weight 300.
spec_violations.AS-036_gradient: Expected NO gradients. Got 2 linearGradient definitions (logoGrad, accentGrad).
spec_violations.AS-036_accent_dot: Expected NO accent dot. Got amber dot with glow filter.
spec_violations.AS-036_separator: Expected none. Got amber gradient separator line.
spec_violations.AS-035_monogram: Expected geometric D/A interlocking monogram (currentColor, single weight, architectural). Got overlapping D and A text elements with slash separator.
spec_violations.AS-035_standalone: File does not exist. No way to use the mark independently.

critical_unaddressed: AS-002 through AS-006: 5 photorealistic hero scenes for product sections (P2)
AS-007 through AS-014: Product screenshots — noted as 'verified existing' but many fail dimension/size specs (P1)
AS-045 through AS-051: Mobile crop variants (P5)

recommendations: REBUILD logo.svg from scratch per AS-035 + AS-036 spec: geometric D/A monogram at 64×64 plus lockup at 240×40, Switzer Medium 500, no gradients, no accent dot, no 'APPS' sub-text, currentColor support
CREATE AS-035 as separate monogram mark SVG (critical path blocker)
REDESIGN favicon.svg per AS-037 — derived from new AS-035 geometry, not the current orange/gold gradient design
CREATE asset-generation-log.md documenting: tool (Mistral FLUX), model (flux-pro), full prompts, processing scripts, date, operator for every generated asset
ADD XMP/comment metadata to SVG files with author and generation method
RE-COMPRESS all screenshots to meet spec budget targets (especially fenix-live-map.png at 8.4× over budget)
RESIZE screenshots to match spec target dimensions or document deviation rationale
LABEL CodexPilot mockups as 'prototype' per AS-011/AS-012 spec requirements
VERIFY 0Brain dashboard is from a live deployment (or label as generated mockup)
ADD GSAP ScrollTrigger integration markers to hero-diagram.svg or split into two separate state SVGs as the spec describes
GENERATE remaining P1/P2 assets before homepage rebuild can be considered complete
